title: The Way of the Diamond Hands
content:
The monk rose before the markets opened. He lit no candles — only charts. For three hundred days he had watched the red and green dance like monks at a funeral, each wick a lesson in impermanence.

He had been wrecked. He had been rugged. He had held through the liquidations of lesser men. And yet he sat.

Today the screen was red again. He placed his palms together, breathed, and watched his portfolio dissolve like incense smoke into the void. Loss is the tuition. The market is the teacher. The monk does not close his position — he deepens his breath.

"This too shall dump," he whispered. "And this too shall pump."
mantra: Buy the fear. Sell the ego. Hold the void.
